title: Replace deprecated `strings.Title` and fix staticcheck warnings
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [ ] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: This PR should fix false-positive golangci-lint errors. Those errors seem to be caused by a cache miss.
tmt tests failed for commit cdd528cca3f4b0676b466c17d2ad01e2a066b7df. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

> I fine with the code changes but personally I do not like the nolintlint changes, a ton of clutter that is hard to maintain understand, i.e. the next person adding //nolint:staticcheck will forget this.
> 

Me neither. We could disable `nolintlint` in `.golangci.yml`, but on the other hand, keeping it forces developers to think carefully before using nolint in their code.

> Do we have reproducer for this? I would rather see if we can fix golangci-lint upstream. cc @kolyshkin

Should I search for CI runs that failed? I think I already lost them.

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/28893 logs one failed run

But I think we already lost the caches for that, I think the next time that happens we need to download the cache somehow and try to reproduce locally in a container or something where can restore the gh actions cache easily

> #28893 logs one failed run
> 
> But I think we already lost the caches for that, I think the next time that happens we need to download the cache somehow and try to reproduce locally in a container or something where can restore the gh actions cache easily

Okay, maybe tomorrow morning. I noticed it on multiple PRs this morning, but then it disappeared. I'll try to get that tomorrow.
I went through the `staticcheck` warnings and either fixed them or added reasons to the `//nolint` comments.
tmt tests failed for commit f1d0ded43cd7a296ee6044781cb94262863af1ef. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

The golangci-lint caching issue is a floating bug, and I've only seen it with staticcheck (maybe even some of its specific checks) and nolintlint, and to my best knowledge it is still not solved (https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/issues/5979, https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/issues/3228). 

This PR LGTM.
